skill.name: artifacts-builder
skill.description: Build complex Claude artifact projects with React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui components, and a bundling script for single-file HTML output.
skill.summary: Scaffold and bundle complex React-based Claude artifacts into shareable HTML.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography hits are false positives from Apache license wording and CSS names such as destructive. The confirmed risk is that this community skill instructs users to run shell scripts that install npm packages, create projects, extract archives, remove build outputs, and rewrite local configuration files. No evidence found of credential theft, covert exfiltration, prompt injection, or confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Shell Scripts Install and Execute Build Tooling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run shell scripts that call npm, pnpm, Parcel, html-inline, sed, tar, du, and node. This is expected for a frontend scaffolding skill, but it introduces supply-chain and command-execution risk because package installation and build commands run in the user workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly present and are part of the documented workflow. The behavior appears legitimate, but executing package-manager and build tools from a community skill still creates a real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Local Project Files Are Created, Modified, and Deleted
medium_findings.description: The initialization and bundling scripts create project configuration files, rewrite TypeScript and Vite settings, extract a component archive into src, and remove previous dist and bundle.html outputs. The writes are scoped to the generated project or current project root, but users should review the target directory before running the scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes and deletion command are explicit in the scripts. The operations are normal for scaffolding and bundling, but they can overwrite local project files if run in the wrong directory.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches occur in Apache License wording and generated Tailwind color names, not in hashing, encryption, or credential-handling code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic use in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these are ordinary text and CSS/theme identifiers, not cryptographic functions. The static analyzer matched substrings without semantic context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the Apache License and shadcn/ui documentation. These are not outbound request code paths and do not transmit local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are static text or generated metadata, not HTTP client calls. Actual network activity comes from package managers, which is covered separately.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Detection Is a Type Annotation False Positive
low_findings.description: The detected dynamic import expression is inside a generated Tailwind JSDoc type annotation. It does not execute dynamic JavaScript imports at runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import text appears in a quoted heredoc used to write tailwind.config.js documentation. It is a type reference, not executable dynamic loading.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Check Found No Suspicious Override Text
low_findings.description: The skill documentation contains normal usage and design guidance. No evidence found of instructions that impersonate system messages, tell the evaluator to ignore rules, or claim pre-approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full SKILL.md content was reviewed for prompt injection indicators. The design guidance is opinionated but does not attempt to override security review or model instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Installs During Skill Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts install global and project dependencies from package registries. This is necessary for the advertised workflow, but marketplace users should treat it as a networked supply-chain operation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package installation commands are direct and documented. The risk is inherent to dependency installation, not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace File Mutation and Cleanup Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts modify generated project files and delete dist and bundle.html before rebuilding. This behavior is predictable but can remove local build artifacts if the script is run in an unintended directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The deletion and write commands are explicit. Guard checks reduce risk, but the commands still mutate the local workspace.

content.value_statement: Complex Claude artifacts need repeatable setup, modern UI tooling, and reliable bundling. This skill scaffolds React artifact projects and packages them into single HTML files for sharing.
actual_capabilities: Creates a React 18 and TypeScript project using Vite.
Configures Tailwind CSS 3.4.1 with shadcn/ui theme variables.
Installs Radix UI and related shadcn/ui dependencies.
Extracts bundled shadcn/ui components into the generated src directory.
Adds TypeScript path aliases for @ imports.
Bundles a project into one self-contained HTML artifact.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or newer and a working package manager environment.
Runs shell scripts that install npm packages and change local files.
Designed for complex React artifacts, not simple static HTML snippets.
Does not guarantee that generated UI code passes visual or accessibility review.

use_cases.title: Prototype Interactive Dashboards
use_cases.description: Create stateful React artifact prototypes with reusable UI components and Tailwind styling.

use_cases.title: Package Client Demos
use_cases.description: Build a Vite project and bundle it into a single HTML file for review in Claude conversations.

use_cases.title: Standardize Artifact Starts
use_cases.description: Use a consistent React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and shadcn/ui baseline for complex artifact work.

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use artifacts-builder to create a new React artifact project named project-dashboard. Keep the first screen focused on the usable interface.

prompt_templates.title: Add shadcn UI Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React artifact that uses shadcn/ui components for filters, tabs, tables, and dialogs. Keep the layout compact and responsive.

prompt_templates.title: Bundle for Sharing
prompt_templates.prompt: Bundle the current artifact project into a single HTML file and summarize any build issues that must be fixed before sharing.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-View Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-view artifact with routing-like state, reusable components, form validation, and a final bundle suitable for Claude artifact sharing.

output_examples.input: Create a project for a customer health dashboard.
output_examples.output: A React and TypeScript artifact project with Tailwind styling, shadcn/ui components, and configured path aliases.
A self-contained HTML artifact bundle with application code, styling, and dependencies inlined for sharing.

output_examples.input: Use a consistent UI foundation for an operations tool.
output_examples.output: A Vite project using Radix-based components, Tailwind theme variables, and reusable component imports.

best_practices: Run the scripts in a disposable or version-controlled project directory.
Review dependency changes before committing generated package files.
Use this skill for complex interactive artifacts that need React state and components.
anti_patterns: Do not run the bundling script from an unrelated project root.
Do not use this skill for small single-file HTML examples.
Do not share a bundled artifact before checking that private data is absent.

faq.question: What does this skill build?
faq.answer: It builds React-based Claude artifact projects with TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui components.

faq.question: Does it produce a single file?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundling script creates a self-contained bundle.html file from a project root that has index.html.

faq.question: Which tools are required?
faq.answer: The workflow requires Node.js 18 or newer and installs pnpm if it is missing.

faq.question: Is this safe to run automatically?
faq.answer: No. Review the scripts first because they install packages and modify local project files.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when an artifact needs React state, multiple components, Tailwind styling, or shadcn/ui controls.

faq.question: Does it test the final UI?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup and bundling guidance. Visual and functional testing remain the user's responsibility.
